text.content:
Total required setup time: 20-30 minutes.

The Afterlife is now open, and the club is popping! It's just another day for Night City's newest gang of edgerunners, on their way to their next job - or is it?

Mission written by _null_.

Danse Macabre

Friend Opportunity: B00zeHound and Jerry

There are two hidden friend opportunities in this mission.

Enemy Opportunity: Maelstrom, Piranhas, or Merrill, Asukaga & Finch

There are three hidden enemy opportunities in this mission.

Rep Opportunity: So Clouseau, Yet So Far

The first time you roll a critical failure to investigate or talk to suspects, fail so hard that you succeed.

Gig Payout

Variable - about 5 Eagles per player.

Beat Chart

Beat 1: Meeting Clarisa

The PCs enter the Afterlife, meet Clarisa, and act as if they are doing downtime at the club. They eventually make it to the bar, where Sammy greets them. Clarisa gets a call and runs off, then returns to the bar for a drink. 'Her song' begins, and she hits the dance floor.

Beat 2: A Nerve Roll From Hell

Clarisa starts dancing wildly, and Sammy or the PCs rush over to help her. She thrashes with an expression of frozen pain as they go to bring her to her room. Sammy says that they shouldn't call Trauma Team until they can get her out of the club. Sprinter tells the players that the owner wants them to figure out what happened, given that Clarisa is a loyal patron and an important income source for the club.

Beat 3: Eye, Meet Magnifying Glass

The players are given free rein to investigate the Afterlife for any clues relating to what happened to Clarisa.

Beat 4: Chasing Leads

On this beat, players can choose to investigate any of the leads that they found while investigating the Afterlife.

  • Beat 4A: The Yogurt Shoal

    Tracking the piranha dealer that's been plugging for Clarisa is a good idea. The players find him at a combat zone frozen yogurt shop that operates as one of the Piranha's 'shoals'.

  • Beat 4B: The Merill, Asukaga, and Finch Building

    Talking to the person Clarisa was talking to before the incident might provide a valuable ally. It looks like they work on the 13th floor of the MA&F building. The players enter the building and find out how they are going to get to the 13th floor.

  • Beat 4C: The Totentanz

    After doing some lead chasing, it looks like there is some kind of Maelstrom connection that connects what happened to Clarisa and a rockerboy named Jerry. The players enter the dangerous Totentanz to get some questions answered.

Beat 5: Aaron Kelly's Bones

AK is Antonio Kelly, Clarisa's new dance instructor. The players confront him at his dance studio on the tip of the Glen to find some answers and get some justice.

The FM Receiver Crackles, The Pirate Wavelength Carrying A Cool, Seductive Female Voice…

"Good evening Night Citizens. This is Nightlight, the voice of the city, here to take the scream out of the sheets and into your ears for those with radio sets and the drive to survive our fair city's streets! No matter how tired from the day you are, the city is still just waking up. Keep your wits up, NC.

"Our top story revolves around the surprising early release of Jerry and The Holofract's latest single: 'Party All Night'. This release is notable due to being unprompted, unheard of, and not even having the right name, with the garden patch page it was hosted on seeming like the song was supposed to be titled 'Mixxer With Misfits'. The patch was taken down two hours after posting, which lends fuel to this being an accidental release. In any case, downloads of the song are available to you incurably curious night owls.

"In other news, I've had people asking non-stop about my hunch that preceded the recent attack on the Central Bank of Night City. Nightcrawlers, come on. I know what is happening in my city, or in this case, what's likely. Keep your ear to the ground for these kinds of scoops, or better yet… keep listening to this broadcast. Who knows? Maybe our next segment concerns you in particular, dear listener.

"Speaking of which, are any of you Night Crawlers superstitious? Do you lock your doors and windows multiple times? Do you throw salt over your shoulder? Do you walk in circles in your apartment, trying to work up the courage to sleep in your container? Reports are coming in throughout Pacifica's residential areas of strange incidents, animal bones on doorsteps, missing neighbors, and chalk symbols in alleyways. If you are not superstitious Night Citizens, it might be a good time to get superstitious soon.

"Coming up next, let's put that ever-present dread aside with some tunes. It's the Jerry and the Holofracts classic from last year, 'Let Me Feel Some Sexual Synergy'. Until next time this has been Nightlight, signing off, and here's hoping I've made your night a little bit brighter."

This mission makes use of the @UUID[Scene.s4clZm7hY4mfQllb]{Afterlife Lobby,} @UUID[Scene.fqgvewCqt8z94mll]{Afterlife Bar & Casino,} @UUID[Scene.Og6KezyP50QPbfHZ]{Afterlife Hotel Rooms,} and the various Danse Macabre maps, along with a few environments. To begin:

In the 7th Circle…

  • Place a token for @UUID[Actor.rQdrdTOYV13yilZD]{Sammy Chauvin} in the bar scene, behind the counter at the bar.

  • Place a token for @UUID[Actor.PJBDw6Qy61QZGIXp]{Silvers} inside the bar, next to the door. She's acting as bouncer and is there to let the players in. She hangs around but avoids idle conversation.

  • Place @UUID[Actor.lyTAAOSCBrk275hV]{Clarisa} in the bar scene, near Sammy's bar.

  • Place the player tokens outside the door, in the hallway beyond.

In the Afterlife Lobby…

  • Place a token for @UUID[Actor.C1opBA4gYUZcTiGp]{Sprinter} at her concierge desk in the lobby.

  • Place a token for @UUID[Actor.lyTAAOSCBrk275hV]{Clarisa} in the lobby, and keep her initially hidden.

  • Place the player tokens at the entrance of the lobby.

  • Change the scene to @UUID[Scene.VCbthP8Ez4uhtdkr]{Afterlife Exterior Environment.}

Then read the following:

Afterlife Exterior - Noir

The time is 10:42 PM, and the rain is sticking to your soles like a Piranha's Smash habit. It isn't quite acidic enough to melt anything, which is a shame - the way this town's going, a little bit of melt would've only been an improvement. The stink of chemicals it holds all the same keeps you from appreciating the uncharacteristic serenity it provides to the dark streets around you, the pitter patter of a million droplets hiding the gunshots in the distance like a black eye hidden under foundation. The rain is cold, reeks of runoff, and blankets all of you as you approach the outside of the Afterlife. Your mission: Sammy the bartender is promising work, and you're eager to stick your hands into the bloody underbelly of Night City once again. Just another late night in the city of dreams.

Change the scene to Afterlife Lobby.

Afterlife Lobby

You all enter the lobby and see Sprinter rubbing the bridge of her nose while flipping through some paperwork. She greets you with a tired smile.

  • Sprinter greets the players and asks after their business in the club that evening.

  • If they ask her what she's doing she responds that she is going through some bills for the Afterlife and organizing them.

    • Normally she would have someone else doing this busy work, but the owner has insisted on keeping the staff size small and limited to essential personnel for now. Since the owner isn't up for the job themself, the responsibility falls on Sprinter.

      • Sprinter says, "Ultimately, these bills are not the real biggest stressor I've been dealing with", and before she can elaborate, she is loudly cut off.

  • Once Sprinter has finished greeting the players, read the following (inspiration: as if you are a lightly tipsy woman at a bachelorette party)

"HEYA, SPRINTER! Who are these gorgeous people?"

You see a pretty woman in a tight chrome-sequinned dress strut towards you. Sprinter's expression is pained and distant for a moment, but she quickly constructs a faux smile and turns to the intruder.

"'Ello Clarisa, how are you?"

  • Clarisa talks to the players excitedly, assuming they are new staff for the club.

  • It should be made clear very quickly to the players that Clarisa is not an edgerunner of any kind, and that she is not aware of the Afterlife's true purpose as a edgerunner meeting place.

    • Any mention of the club's true purpose is interrupted by Sprinter, who tells Clarisa that they are 'previous guests who are looking for work' (unless they have already constructed a lie).

  • Clarisa eventually leaves the conversation, heading towards the club proper.

  • When asking Sprinter about Clarisa, she replies:

"You know how this place used to be a regular hotel, right? Well, that was the hotel's previous landlady who sold this building to our current owner. She's… well… a bit of a euro-heiress ditz, but she overpays and overtips for everything at the club, so the owner's decided to let her stay in her own part of the hotel. She's harmless enough, just a bit irritating. Whenever she's in the club, just keep her happy and stay out of earshot when talking about 'work', k?"

  • After conversation concludes, Sprinter will encourage the players to head to check in with Sammy for their job.

Return to the 7th Circle

The players re-enter the 7th Circle, and they see Sammy subtly smile at their arrival. They also see Clarisa at Sammy's bar, playfully flirting with him before she gets a call from her agent. She looks at it, and the players might notice (Human Perception DV11, autopass with 3 or more points) her expression shift from playful fun to a more sober, frustrated look. She walks off to a private corner and takes her call.

  • If any of the players decide to listen in on this call, they hear the following call (but only from Clarisa's end):

?: Clarisa, finally.

Clarisa: What is it? I’m busy.

?: We need to talk finances babe, you're bleeding money right now.

Clarisa: I’m not bleeding anything. You’re the one who’s gonna be bleeding if you keep calling me on my day off.

?: Look, I get it, but your inheritance can only keep you going so long.

Clarisa: Did you check that number?  If not, double-check it. That’s a cool 750K, and that’s after a couple of years. I’ve been careful, and I'm allowed to destress how I want. You sound like my mom.

?: Claire…

Clarisa: Don’t you Claire me! You know what would really save me something? Cutting you off. Don’t call again tonight, and I'll give you some cash tomorrow.

?: Ugh. Fine, boss.

Clarisa: You bet your… ugh. Look, just talk to me tomorrow, okay?

-END CALL-

After her convo, Clarisa hangs up her agent and returns to the bar smiling. She will ask Sammy for her 'usual' (a Triti-Fizz and Vodka) which she takes, swirls a few times, and then drinks. The song 'PARTY ALL NIGHT' starts playing, at which Clarisa yells "This is my song!" before rushing over to the dance floor.

  • Sammy will use this opportunity to talk to the players about the gig that he had for them.

    • You can make up whatever you want for this faux gig! Get creative and make sure that, whatever it is, it keeps the players talking for a bit and that it is low-stakes. You can even have your players do your seeded gig after this one if you want to inject some more of your personality into the campaign. If you cannot think of anything, here are some suggestions:

      • A new 'Alien Dissection' tourist trap has opened up in the badlands, and there are suspicions of Scav connections to the establishment.

      • Sightings of the Night City Gator have re-emerged, and Biotechnica is offering a decent sum to the group that can capture and deliver it to them.

      • Two warring nomad packs are competing in a game of Stickball, and they need impartial referees and backup players.

  • While talking about the gig, Sammy suddenly gets very quiet before muttering "Huh?" under his breath. Read the following.

Sammy looks out at the flashing lights of the club behind you, where Clarisa went to dance.

"Hey, Clarisa? You good?" he calls out.

Clarisa doesn't respond, but then, it's loud in here.

"Clarisa?", he calls again before squinting.

When you turn to look at her, you see her flailing her arms and legs in strange, fast ways. You might just dismiss her as drunk at first - until you catch the pained and frozen expression of horror on her face, illuminated only by the lightly flashing lights on the dance floor.

The group now has free reign to explore the Afterlife for clues.

Character Knowledge:

The people in the Afterlife know more about Clarisa, and they might be able to provide more context to what just happened.

Sammy Chauvin:

Sammy is shocked, and Clarisa's main advocate against the cold focus of Silvers and the business-focused attitude of Silvers. He gives any information he has willingly, but he admits to not knowing much.

  • Sammy knows that Clarisa has frequented the bar from day one of 7th Circle's opening and that he has seen her talking to Sprinter and the staff before it opened officially.

  • Clarisa's 'usual' was a Raspberry Lime Vodka Soda, made with Triti-fizz.

  • Sammy knows that Clarisa does some recreational drugs, specifically synth-coke and molly. She also mentioned that she had a Piranha plug at somewhere called the 'Yogurt Shoal'.

  • Clarisa would sometimes talk about her dance instructor Antonio, and her accountant Sasha.

  • Clarisa would playfully flirt with Sammy, but never with any real intent or genuine interest. Sammy remembers sex being a bit of a sore subject with her, one that she would quickly try to move on from.

Sprinter:

Sprinter is unnerved, but still focused on what best serves the Afterlife.

  • She knows that the owner and Clarisa have known each other for a while and while the owner does not consider Clarisa a peer, they do seem to have some kind of sympathy or care for her. Sprinter does not know why and does not want to ask.

  • Sprinter remembers that Clarisa would bring a 'shy, bin-wearing* girl' over to the Afterlife regularly, but she has not seen that shy girl in the last few weeks.

    • *Bin is British for glasses. British people are weird, huh?

  • Sprinter is not too hellbent on revenge, given that she disliked Clarisa quite a bit. Clarisa's unusual habits and free reign of some of the hotel bugged Sprinter. Sprinter also notes that Clarisa has been a bit more unstable these past few weeks.

  • Sprinter got the song that played during Clarisa's incident off of the Data Pool after she heard about it being released early. Sprinter then added it to the hours-long digital DJ playlist without really thinking about it again.

  • The band that made the song, Jerry and the Holofracts, is a noted raver/party rock 'group'. Technically the band is just one guy (the eponymous Jerry) with the other members being holographic vocaloids.

  • The full list of members is:

    • Jerry, the real artist, singer, and frontman

    • Jimmy, the focused and skilled keyboardist

    • Jason, the bad-boy (don't play by the rules) guitarist

    • Jeremy, the intense and scary drummer

Silvers:

Silvers is unfazed and she avoids answering any questions beyond necessary ones. She is guarding and monitoring Clarisa (sending some other staff to keep bouncing the 7th Circle).

  • Clarisa's state is slowly improving, but the thrashing has not stopped.

  • Silvers knows that Clarisa was regularly going to the Glen border zone(the edge of the Glen that bleeds into the combat zone) at least twice a week. She knows this because Clarisa would occasionally ask for defense tips, to which Silvers would reply, "Tip one, go somewhere else."

  • Silvers does say that Clarisa has been of some help in the past. She has a good relationship with some Piranhas that have allowed the club to acquire some 'useful goods'.

  • Silvers thinks that Sprinter dislikes Clarisa because her euro-heiress status especially reminds her of some tumultuous events from her past. Silvers admits to being occasionally annoyed by the woman, but she holds no anger towards her.

Clues in Clarisa's Room:

Her Wrist Agent, Electronics/Security Tech check (Can also unlock all info via her password: A11N1GHT)

DV 10

What are her contacts?

  • Sasha(accountant)

  • B00zeHound (piranha supplier)

  • Antonio(dance)

  • M&D (mom and dad)

DV 13

Can we get a transcript of the call?

Yes. (Play the call transcript from Beat One)

DV 16

What is her schedule?

  • Sunday: PARTY in the morning, prep at night.

  • Monday: Go to work at the Glen.

  • Tuesday: Work, Meet with Antonio

  • Wednesday: Work, Meet with M&D for euro

  • Thursday: Work, Spa day at night (pick up party supplies).

  • Friday: PARTY TIME!

  • Saturday: P.A.R.T.Y.

Her body, Cybertech or Deduction check.

DV 10

What caused this?

The music must have triggered the effect, not any drugs in her drink.

DV 13

What ware does she have?

She is chipped with a braindance recorder, any-fit feet, and a cyberaudio suite (if this was found with Cybertech, the player notices that her cyberaudio has been hacked). Her braindance recorder is running, weirdly

DV 16

Who did this?

It must have been some kind of hypnotist or chemical. She does not have sufficient ware to have her body act like this due to a hack.

Her room, Conceal/Reveal or Deduction check.

DV 10

What can I glean about Clarisa based on this room? 

The room is messy, with clothes thrown in a pile next to a hamper. Her makeup vanity is well organized in stark contrast. She cared much more about how she looked outside of this room.

DV 13

Does she have anything hidden here?

Nothing earth-shattering. A drawer of sex toys, a work computer, a list of dates. It becomes clear that she holds multiple jobs, one being a stock market trader and another being a braindance porn star.

DV 16

Who else has been here?

At first, it seems like just her… but the bottom of the trash can reveals the truth. Used condoms from at least a week ago.

Clues at the 7th Circle:

Her Drink: Triti-Fizz and Vodka, Science(Chemistry) check.

DV 10

Is it spiked? 

Yes, the drink has small traces of the powdered foam of a recently crushed pill.

DV 13

What is it spiked with?

Half a Molly, ground.

DV 16

What is the dosage? 

70mg(slightly below standard).

DV 20

Is there some other chemical here?

Nearly imperceptible to anyone, something about the smell confuses you.

The song, Composition or Play Instrument check.

DV 10

Have I heard this song before? 

Not in a club, but it has been gaining traction in the garden. It does not sound like what usually plays at the Afterlife.

DV 13

Who wrote this? Who performed this?

Jerry and the Holofracts, a popular garden patch band that makes raver music. It is unknown where they get their funds from. Some strange, static-like background noise plays in the track.

DV 17

My fingers trace the chords. What is being hidden here? 

Heavy bass, screaming frenzy… chromer rock. Your hands reveal the truth between the chords. This sound is the sound of the Totentanz, the sound of Maelstrom.

At this point, the players will have three major leads they can follow up on (depending on what clues that they find):

  1. Clarisa has someone named @UUID[Actor.F8eeHrvoA08QvPxL]{B00zeHound} on her agent, who is a known Piranha dealer. Maybe they know something? (Location: @UUID[Scene.Bzh48OjmFSyyn5SQ]{Yogurt Shoal)}

  2. Clarisa was taking a phone call before her episode. Looking up the number, it looks like it was from a Merril, Asukaga, and Finch accountant. (Location: @UUID[Scene.uhY2vLgFlM4dA8V3]{Merril, Asukaga, and Finch)}

  3. The song reminds you of the Totentanz. Perhaps it might be a good idea to chat up some Maelstromers. (Location: @UUID[Scene.3F46oncmINIuqf5l]{Totentanz)}

Each of these leads will lead to a different scene (or scenes), so encourage your players to go off and investigate.

Setting up the Aaron Kelly Dance Studio: The players should be placed in the @UUID[Scene.MYQDJzWfelBf8JsK]{Aaron Kelly Studio Floor One} scene, on the street outside the building. There is a bodega to the north of the studio with a tired man in it. Players can buy anything in the CRB that is cheaper than 50 Eurobucks here (including ammo).

Then, switch yourself to the @UUID[Scene.vns3jR3C8M5Hpccp]{Aaron Kelly Studio Floor Two} scene and place @UUID[Actor.qkQNXPG8OHhnkpXN]{Antonio Kelly} at the area in the apartment with the red stains on the floor. Also place your players at the base of the staircase for later. Return to the Floor One scene and activate it.

How That Dead Man Danced

The studio is dark. Located on the edge of the Glen and Old Combat Zone, the not-distant-enough gunshots of the zone ring out from the alley behind you. The studio itself sits at the bottom of a two-story building, with the logo of a dancing corpse painted on its window.

The building is conventionally locked (DV: 15 Pick Lock), and faint music and light filter out of the second-story windows (DV: 17 Perception). The window between the street and studio has an sdp of 30, and it will start a faint alarm when broken. This alarm can be turned off with a DV: 13 Electronics/Security Tech check.

Important: If the alarm is signalled, it will alert Antonio Kelly to the players. Upstairs, he will hide and try to ambush them. If the alarm is left on, the police will arrive on the scene to investigate in roughly five minutes.

There is nothing of particular note in the dance studio, and it seems surprisingly normal. The players can find the following things by exploring:

  • A dry-erase calendar near the door that lists different clients/classes at different times each day. There are even general seniors/children's classes listed. Clarisa is listed at 6:00-7:20 on Tuesdays, and it looks like she's taking solo classes. The last part where her name shows up is next Tuesday, where she is booked with several other girls for a 'Group Performance'. Weirdly, her name stops being listed afterward.

  • A switch that changes the window of the studio from clear to fully grey and opaque. Next to it is a light switch. On a DV: 15 Basic Tech, it is apparent that the light switch has been modified to have a secret third setting between 'on' and 'off'. When this third setting is active, the lights start humming and flickering in an odd pattern.

  • Exercise balls and other equipment in the back storage room, along with shoes and towels.

  • A framed photo on the wall of Antonio Kelly and his father Aaron Kelly. On a DC: 20 Deduction or Perception check, the outline of scribbles on the backside of the photo can be seen. Taking the photo out of the frame reveals the back message to read:

"Aaron and Ant at the Richard Night Aquarium, 2022.

hey dad"

The door between the studio and the stairs up is also locked (being a DV: 13 Pick Lock check). The players can find a back locker room under the stairs, which contains a medtech bag at the back and assorted dance clothes in the lockers. (A DV: 15 conceal/reveal object check nets a forgotten wallet belonging to a 'Terry Magdiline Hartweather' with a SIN card, a credit card and 43 Eurobucks).

If the players decide to climb the stairs, switch to the Floor Two scene.

A Private Last Dance

Music and multi-colored light faintly streams out of the bottom of the door at the end of the hallway. The music is that of a slow dance, slow drifting R&B. As you walk forward, you are each hit by a horrible smell. Like rotting rose petals, lube, spoiled milk and forgotten takeout. Your faces scrunch up reflexively.

IF THE ALARM WAS NOT SOUNDED:

You open the door to see a wrecked apartment. Trash and refuse litter the floor. At the other end of the room you see a man scrawling something on the wall with his bloodied hands, his back turned to you. The message on the wall reads "PARTY ALL NIGHT" in large letters. The man is shirtless, and wearing a huge disco-ball-shaped helmet that is slowly spinning and spreading shimmering light around the room. As the door opens, he stops his scrawling and turns to look at you, his face fully obscured by the helmet.

"Its really late… do you? do you have an… appointment? haha… no need for anyone to… to get hurt. lets talk?"

Antonio stands up, waiting for the players to make a move. He is willing to talk openly with the players (go to what he knows), but will attack the instant he feels threatened. His disco helmet works similarly to a Superflash Jacket, and he will activate it before/during his first turn in combat.

IF THE ALARM WAS SOUNDED:

You open the door to see a wrecked apartment. Trash and refuse litter the floor. At the other end of the room, you see a bloody message on the wall that reads "PARTY ALL NIGHT" in large letters.

Have the players make a group perception check after entering the door (tell solos to add threat detection if they have any points in that). The DV for this roll is 15. Antonio will attack the player with the lowest roll using his Maelstrom mod-wolvers, regardless of success. If the player did succeed, they are able to evade this attack. If more than half of the party succeeded on the perception roll, initiative is rolled. Otherwise, treat this as an ambush.

Antonio knows the following:

QUICK NOTE: If the players kill Antonio before he is able to divulge some of the more important info, you can store that info in an encrypted journal or locked agent (DV: 13 Cryptography and DV:13 Electronics/Security Tech respectively) so that the players can still finish the job on a high note.

  • He has been supplying Clarisa and several other women with private dance classes, during which he has been subliminally hypnotizing them with the altered lighting system. He has been targeting attractive women with braindance implants, and he has found three.

  • He is planning on using the commissioned song from Jerry as an 'activator' to bring on the 'endless dance'. He and several other Maelstrom gangers are doing this all to set up for a 'private party' with the girls, then once they are done they are going to sell the XBDs of the party to get some eddies and finish it all out by selling the girls to the Weng Fang Tong.

  • He has been struggling with hypnotizing Clarisa in particular, because of her chaotic lifestyle and substance use. To fix this, he tracked down her dealer (B00zeHound) and made him apply a barbiturate-based hypnotic-enhancing drug cocktail.

  • The only way to end the effect is to flash the lights in a specific pattern (Off 2 secs, Flash, Flash, Off 2 secs, Flash, Flash, Flash, Off 3 secs, On).

  • He does not know that the song was released online and that Clarisa has been triggered early. He freaks out when he learns this and gains a -2 to all of his rolls for the next 5 minutes.

After Party:

After subduing Antonio Kelly and searching for any remaining information they need, the players can return to the Afterlife to share their findings.

Correct solutions to earlier questions:

  • They need to give a detailed explanation of the events that led to Clarisa's affliction (If they forget minor details, they can make DV: 13 Concentration checks to get hints).

  • They can use the light pattern to end the effect on Clarisa.

  • They can name Antonio Kelly as the definite culprit. They can also name B00zeHound and Maelstrom as collaborators (optional).

Sprinter takes the information that the players give her, then immediately goes to try their proposed treatment on Clarisa. If they got the light sequence right, she stops thrashing around and snaps out of her dazed state. She then starts crying and thanks the players and Sprinter with a hug. Sprinter awkwardly accepts the hug, and the players are provided their eagles.

Your Feedback Wanted!

Did you enjoy this session? Did something go wrong? Was there broken content? I would very much like to hear about your successes and failures both.

If you have a few minutes after your session, please take the time to fill out the feedback survey for this campaign, located here. It should only take 5 minutes, and will be actively considered as I make revisions to the campaign.

Your feedback matters! Thank you for playing!

This scene makes use of the @UUID[Scene.Og6KezyP50QPbfHZ]{Afterlife Hotel Rooms} scene. To begin:

  • Place @UUID[Actor.lyTAAOSCBrk275hV]{Clarisa's} token on the bed in the suite on the bottom right corner of the map. This is Clarisa's room.

  • Place the player tokens within the room.

  • Place relevant Afterlife staff in the room.

Remain in the 7th Circle Bar scene after doing so, for now.

Panic on the Dance Floor

Sammy rushes out from behind the bar, calling for Silvers. The lights come on and the music stops as the two try to grab Clarisa who is dancing manically with a strange, frozen look of fear on her face. She does not respond to any stimuli and she continues to thrash around even when put on the ground.

  • Subduing Clarisa requires a DV15 First Aid check or a DV13 Paramedic check, and restraining her requires a DV13 Brawling check.

    • Silvers will assist how she can.

    • Once Clarisa has been subdued, Silvers calls Sprinter on her agent, informs her what's happened, and tells her to talk to the owner.

  • If the players suggest calling Trauma Team or otherwise remove Clarisa from the premises, Silvers will tell them to wait until they ask the owner.

    • Silvers' Justification: This is only the first few days of the Afterlife's opening, and everything's run smoothly so far. The last thing the club needs is Trauma showing up or word getting out that someone's been assassinated in the bar.

    • Sammy objects to this, but he ultimately backs down and does what Silvers says, and encourages the players to do the same.

      • If the players still insist on immediately moving Clarisa elsewhere, have Sprinter intervene by notifying them through their agents that they'll be paid to figure out what's going on and to keep a lid on the situation.

    • Once the situation has calmed enough to resolve itself, Silvers brings Clarisa to her room.

Clarisa's Room

Clarisa's room is messier than you expected, but not overwhelmingly so. Nice clothes are strewn about the floor, and a small trash can in the corner can just barely contain the packed garbage stored within it. Her small SmartVanity is, by contrast, meticulously organized and clean. Silvers lays Clarisa's shaking body on her bed and then she steps out of the room.

Sammy is deeply shaken, and he commiserates with the players about the startling and strange situation. Clarisa's still thrashing around with the same frozen expression, but she is not getting out of the bed and her vitals seem to be fine outside of dilated pupils and an elevated heart rate.

After a moment, Sprinter walks into the room. Even she is surprised about seeing the state Clarisa is in. Sprinter tells the players that she and Silvers filled in the owner, who has decided to keep Clarisa under watch in her room unless her symptoms get any more serious. The owner wants to keep Trauma Team away for the moment, as they want the club's operating season to go as smoothly as possible and with minimal potential media coverage. The owner has also decided to personally hire the players to figure out what happened to Clarisa and why before they have to bring her to the hospital in the morning.

Job Details

  • The group has roughly Twelve Hours (It is 10:30 pm now and they are going to bring her to the hospital at 10:30 am tomorrow) to solve the following:

    • What Happened to Her?

      • Detailed explanation and timeline

    • How Can We Stop It?

      • Treatments, cures, etc.

    • Who Is Responsible?

      • First Name, Last Name, detailed information and confirmation

  • The time limit is in place so that the owner can reach out to some media friends of theirs early with enough information to beat out any potential negative press that might get linked back to the bar. The owner also has a vested interest in Clarisa getting better, given that she is a reliable patron.

  • After getting solid answers to all three questions, they are to report to Sprinter with their findings. Sprinter will then look through all the information themselves and have it sent to the medias that the owner works with. If the group's information clashes with the media's own investigation, it will severely… 'disappoint' the owner and halve their payout.

  • Given that this is an internal matter, the owner has pledged to give up to 5 Eagles to each member of the party, depending on how successful they are.

  • If the group finds a guilty party, they are encouraged to deal with them as they see fit. They will not get a bonus payout for neutralizing the guilty party. Sammy chimes in saying he'll front them a round of drinks for the trouble though, a notion which Sprinter shrugs at but doesn't fight back against.

Setting up the @UUID[Scene.Bzh48OjmFSyyn5SQ]{Yogurt Shoal:} The players should be placed in the Yogurt Shoal scene, outside of the building. Make sure the @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.chaw6SnD8fe0ix6E]{three Piranha gangers} are in one of the booths, and that @UUID[Actor.F8eeHrvoA08QvPxL]{B00zeHound} is behind the counter. Put three @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.9WT6eQPP522T77mq]{street junkies} in front of B00zeHound, and then populate the street with junkies, punks, and bullies to your hearts desire.

(Note, for maximum chaos roll on the Cyberpunk Red corebook's random encounter table, making sure to adjust with higher numbers given that the players are in a combat zone. This is fully optional, but can serve as a good choice if you want to sell the 'danger' of the zones.)

Swimming into Yogurt Shoal

You step into the infamously crammed combat zone street known as Los Lobos Street (also called wolf street) by the locals. The street is filled to the brim with junkies and various other people down on their luck. The street stinks of vomit and gasoline, with the single frozen yogurt shop serving as a stark contrast to the gloom. The words 'Yogurt Shoal' are inscribed via a neon sign that also contains an image of a cartoon fish swimming into a frozen yogurt cup.

A combat zone frozen yogurt shop (on the corner of Pacific Blvd and Los Lobos St) that serves as one of the Piranha’s ‘Shoals’. The Piranahs will see anyone who chooses to come to their door, as long as that person is willing to talk biz. This building does not have a net architecture.

The players can duck into the back alley and mess with the building's generator if they wish to gain more of a 'surprise attack'.

You walk up to the yogurt shop, passing under the neon sign. You see who you presume to be B00zeHound at the counter, wearing a small paper hat and a raggedy cream-brown trench. He is talking to two girls and a male joytoy, his chrome grill glittering as he speaks. The group all laughs together before leaving the shop. In the booths to your right, three quiet Piranha members sit patiently. One of them is smoking. B00zeHound turns over and smiles.

(Read in a relaxed, friendly voice.)

"Welcome to the Yogurt Shoal, pal. Looking for some late-night treats?"

Dishing with B00zeHound:

B00zeHound smiles as the players approach, and is very open… until the players ask about Clarisa. 

  • He can be persuaded at a high difficulty. DV: 18. 

  • Trying to fast talk him with a Conversation is much more effective. DV: 15.

  • The players can learn more about him through Human Perception or Deduction. DV:17

  • The players can try to also try to Bribe him. DV: 16 - (euro offered by players / 100) rounded up.

Should the players commit a serious faux-pas, or try and push him to talk about too much too quickly, he will gesture over at the Piranah goons in the both and attempt a facedown against one of the players. If all else fails, iron is drawn and initiative is rolled.

During a fight, B00zeHound will attempt to run the moment one of the piranha goons drops, doing anything he can to make it off the bounds of the map and escape.

He knows some things he gives freely:

  • Clarisa was one of his top clients, and she had been coming to him for the last four years.

  • She, despite all of her constant partying, was a lot more measured than his other clients. She planned out where she would be, portioned her money, and always brought friends with her to places. That all changed these last few weeks, as Clarisa had started going off the ‘deep end’. 

And he knows some things that he will not:

  • He has an arrangement with some Maelstromer guy, where the guy drops off a list of ‘clients’ that need their drugs spiked with some kind of ‘secret sauce’. B00zeHound feels partially bad about doing it, but he really feels like he needs the money.

  • B00zeHound describes the Maelstromer guy as 'Wearing a strange disco ball helmet, with spiky clothing and a reedy, athletic build' .

  • Clarisa was on this list and the Maelstromer was insistent that she, in particular, got some of this ‘secret sauce’.

  • B00zeHound, against the terms of his agreement with the booster, did some chem analysis on this ‘secret sauce’. It seemed to be a heavily diluted mix of barbituates and Veritas. Undetectable when included in a pill’s composition.

Setting up the MA&F Building: The players should be placed in the @UUID[Scene.uhY2vLgFlM4dA8V3]{MA&F Lobby} scene, right at the entrance. Pepper the lobby scene and the MA&F office scene with some @UUID[Actor.oXJZUUkyafqAzCdb]{MA&F Corpos} and put four @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.AIR0MIWchnbMvM5D]{Rat-Sec Grunts} in the lobby (two in the lobby proper, the other two in the back area behind the desk). Make sure there is one MA&F Corpo behind the reception desk.

Balancing Budgets:

You enter the Merill, Asukaga, and Finch offices, located at the center of the 'mostly' clean Glen district. Very close to city hall, the Office tower stands as a testament to the finance corps' strength. The lobby itself is very clean and open, and you all see corpos chatting with each other and various well-endowed clientele at the small raised glass tables and seats around the glass window edge of the lobby.

This building can get very dangerous to edgerunners without good social sense. Anyone who is wearing gang colors, bag lady chic, or nomad leathers is assumed to not belong and is a check away from having security called on them. This also applies to characters with visible armor or weaponry.

IMPORTANT: While the RatSec Grunts might not serve a challenge to the players any grunt or exec can use an action to alert the NCPD, who arrive in 1d6 rounds. RatSec grunts and execs will only do this once at least two RatSec grunts have dropped. The Glen serves as the location of the NCPD's Central Precinct, which means that 5 NCPD Patrol officers arrive with 2 NCPD Enforcer Groundcars. If NCPD units are dropped, they will continue to call backup so be wary.

Talking to the receptionist, the players have a few ways to talk to Sasha:

  • If they know which floor she works at (the 13th), they can schedule an account sign-up appointment with one of the accountants on that floor without needing to make any rolls. (This costs 200eb)

  • They can ask for an appointment with Sasha, at which point that character will need to make a Luck Save (roll 1d10 and try to get the result under the total of their current Luck points) to see if she is available at this time of night.

  • They can pretend to be Rat Sec Accountants or Executives themselves, which can only be attempted if one of the party members is wearing businesswear and they succeed on a DV: 13 personal grooming check. This will give the player(s) attempting to deceive the Receptionist the ability to make a DV: 17 acting check. If any of the players got higher than a 17 on their earlier personal grooming check, they gain a +2 to the acting roll.

  • They can attempt to bribe the receptionist DV: 17 - (euro offered by players / 200) rounded up.

  • They can have a netrunner hack into the building's system and set a fake appointment for them.

  • They can forge a note from Clarisa saying that they are talking to Sasha in her place. DV: 17

  • They can threaten the receptionist through a facedown (Note, this is VERY risky. RatSec escorts them out on failure, or if they are too loud with their intimidation).

Water Cooler Talk:

The players can ride the elevator up to the 13th floor, where they eventually find Sasha in one of the small office rooms, which has her name engraved on a door plaque.

The moment you lay eyes on who you assume to be Sasha, you see a tired woman with a short pixie cut and a light blue cardigan on. She has a mug of Koff-pop mocha 3 at her side, and a small compression brace on her right hand. She is scrolling through her computer when you enter, clearly doing some complicated financial math. She looks up from her computer at all of you.

If the players manage to schedule an appointment with her, she has a chipper facade and treats the group as new potential account holders. Otherwise, she will be curiously surprised and will proceed to ask who the players are. The moment Clarisa is brought up, Sasha will get extremely worried. A DV: 13 persuasion check is required to convince her that you are telling her the truth about Clarisa. This roll can be given additional +1 modifiers for each piece of evidence that the players can provide. Once the players succeed, Sasha will tell the players anything they want and she will help as much as she can. If the players fail, Sasha will call security and immediately try to contact Clarisa.

Here is what Sasha knows:

  • Clarisa was taking out regular sums of money for ‘personal use’. When asked by Sasha about it, Clarisa jokingly said “One for my dealer, one for my dancer, one for my landlady, and one for me.”

  • Sasha knows about B00zeHound and has even met him once.

  • She has never met Antonio Kelly, but she knows that he works at the Aaron Kelly Dance Studio.

  • Sasha and Clarisa have been hooking up, and Sasha has even been at the Afterlife Hotel a couple of times. Of course, Sasha has never been in the club areas and all she knows is that it’s a hotel that Clarisa owned, sold, and is still living in.

  • Clarisa has been talking a lot about Antonio, her instructor. Secretly Sasha feels a bit jealous, but not enough to feel any anger towards the guy. Sasha remembers Clarisa talking a bit about how much Antonio keeps trying to get her to go to the Totentanz. Antonio talks about it being, “the epicenter of REAL dance”.

  • Clarisa's finances are a bit wild. She gets released roughly four thousand a week for regularly interacting with her parents, and she has about seven-hundred and thirty-five thousand stored in the bank. The problem is that, given Clarisa's current party-heavy lifestyle, she started a habit of losing roughly ten thousand a week in strange benders. This coupled with her regular rent payments meant that she has been draining her fortune at an exponentially increasing rate.

  • Clarisa's parents are old, old money from Austria. Her last name, and the last names of her parents, is Bohm. Clarisa dislikes her parents quite a bit, and she seems to resent them for keeping her very sheltered from the world. She has, over her seven years in the city, been lying to them about her current life status and acting as if she has been a 'paragon of virtue' every time they call.

Setting up the @UUID[Scene.3F46oncmINIuqf5l]{Totentanz:} The players should be placed in the Totentanz scene, right at the elevator at the bottom left. Pepper the hallway with street trash (tier 1 street thugs) and place a Maelstrom-affiliated @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.siXtBrzjkX0N0AIc]{Gangster Enforcer} by the door. In the club proper, place roughly 10 civvies and street trash outside the stage. Place 2 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.CMi960vZd9O0kTUd]{maelstrom rippers} on stage, and 3 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.4xU3a21yd0HN77KT]{maelstrom brawlers} at the table south of the bar. Place a @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.hyIBMpK21BmlaAU4]{street punk} behind the bar, @UUID[Actor.jz0AoU38jx6qbCN1]{Jerry} at one of the booths south of the bar, and lastly put a maelstrom psycho in the back area of the club.

Let's Bounce:

You enter the run-down building and being to ride the sweaty elevator up to the Totentaz. Used condoms, trash, and various small brown stains freckle the floor of this elevator, which weakly sticks to your shoes as you step on it. The elevator arrives at one of the top floors before opening, and muffled, thumping chromer music greets your ears. A line of various chromers, punks, and lost tourists stretches before you towards the entrance of the Totentanz.

This building can get very VERY dangerous to edgerunners without good social sense. Luckily, the dress code is less strict than in places like MA&F. Anyone wearing businesswear or high fashion who approaches the bouncer gets a chuckle and a 'VIP' ticket to the back area. They are escorted by the Maelstrom brawlers at the booth near the door towards the back area, where they (and the Maelstrom psycho back there) will try to kill them and take all their stuff.

Maelstrom does entry at the door, and doesn't do reservations. The entry cost is 25 euro at the door. Players can choose to wait in line (Pass time by 1 hour) or they can choose to cut in line (roll a social check against the Bouncer).

  • Pretend to be Maelstrom Members through a DV: 17 Acting roll.

  • Charm the bouncer with a DV: 15 persuasion roll.

  • Intimidate the bouncer with a Facedown.

  • Bribe the bouncer. DV: 15 - (euro offered by players / 100)

Wearing gang colors (that do not bear the symbols of the Inquisitors or 6th Street) grants a +1 to any of these social rolls. Wearing maelstrom colors/symbols grants an additional +1.

(Dead) Body Shots:

Thumping music, thrashing patrons, a filthy bar with filthier drinks. The floor is sweaty, the air is warm, and the overhead lights hurt your eyes.

However the party enters the club, they see a bunch of civvies/chromers on the dance floor, a bartender at the bar, some maelstrom gangers performing on the stage, and a lonely man sitting at one of the booths. If the players have done any research on Jerry from Jerry and the Holograms, they recognize him at the booth.

The players can ask these different groups for answers.

The Civvies:

  • Don't know shit.

  • Most are high on synth-coke.

  • Will generally ignore the players unless they do something crazy.

The Bartender:

  • The bartender is behind a bulletproof glass case around the bar, almost as if they are a fish in a tank. They slide drinks under small holes at the base of the glass.

  • The bartender avoids talking to patrons, usually only asking what drinks people want.

  • The bartender knows Jerry, and how he performs here every few nights. If the players earn the bartender's trust, they might tell them about Jerry being indebted to Maelstrom or surface level observations he has about the workings of the club/Maelstrom.

The Maelstromers:

  • Don't even try. They will either tell you to shove off or invite you to 'talk' in the back room if you say something alarming to them.

  • If you go to the back room, you get instantly attacked once you're out of the sight of Totentanz patrons.

Jerry:

Jerry is wearing dimmed holo-wear, that is set to look like inconspicuous brown clothes. His face looks really tired, and long blonde hair hangs down to his shoulders and obscures his eyes. He is looking out at the stage, running his finger around the rim of his glass.

On a successful DV: 13 Wardrobe and Style/Deduction check, a carefully made-up black eye on his left and several hidden cuts can be made out. He is drinking by himself, staring off into the distance and trying to blend into the area around him.

Jerry avoids chatting out loud, as to stay out of trouble with Maelstrom. He can be convinced to talk by:

  • Trying to persuade him. DV: 21.

  • Interrogating him. DV: 13.

  • Making small talk with him (Persuasion). DV: 17

  • Buying him a drink (+2 to any other social roll)

Jerry knows the following:

  • He has no idea who Clarisa is. He also has no idea who B00zeHound or Sarah is.

  • He has been indebted to Maelstrom for the past few years for their assistance with his career. This arrangement has been mutually beneficial, until recently. One of the Maelstrom members, 'AK' has started tightening the 'leash' of his arrangement with Maelstrom.

  • AK and some of the other members commissioned a song from Jerry that included this weird static-ey noise to it's backing track. When he asked about the song's purpose, he was only told about it being needed for a 'private party' that would be held 'next Tuesday' (a few days from now).

  • Jerry slipped up while working on the song earlier today. He was high, and he accidentally uploaded it on the garden patch page where he had meant to upload a song meant for the public. Needless to say, Maelstrom was upset at him.

  • AK, who spearheaded the commission offer, hasn't seemed to find out about the song mix-up yet. Jerry is not excited for when he finds out.

  • Jerry's description of AK lines up with B00zeHound's description of 'That Maelstromer Guy'.

  • Jerry says that, AK and his friends are the really freaky members of Maelstrom. He recounts the following story.

"So, AK right? Theres this story one of the gangers told me once about him. Ya see, AK don't got any visible Ware, so everybody wonders how he got so high in Malestrom. The story goes that, he was dared to kill someone close to him as an initiation. The leaders expected him to maybe whine and give up, but thats the thing man. They say he broke into an abandoned industrial mill a few miles out, hung up a bunch of decorations, and filmed himself dancing as a convayor belt dragged his tied up father towards this industrial woodchipper… He aimed the wood chipper right over him, and kept dancing as the blood, bone, and organs of his pops showered his body. Real fucking fever dream XBD shit. When the maelstrom leaders questioned the voracity of the vid, the story goes that AK smiled at them… before pulling out a bag and dumping the only solid remnants of his dad at their feet. Real fucking psycho."

So, Someone Drawed Iron.

So, lets say that something goes wrong. If weapons are drawn in the open club, the Maelstrom gangers will attempt a facedown or defer the task to the bouncer. If the party is packing serious visible firepower, the gangers will try to persuade them to leave before popping off.

The moment a fight breaks out, roll 1d10. This represents how many civvies are going to run away during the fight. Civvies that are choosing to run away join initiative collectively, at the end of the turn order. On the civvies turns, they will do anything to get out of the club and call the elevator. (This includes the people in the line, how much of which being up to the GM).

The elevator takes 3 rounds to arrive, and 3 rounds to reach the bottom floor. There are no other ways to safely exit the Totentanz. Players could always try some unsafe methods of escape (jumping out windows) if they are unable to wait for the elevator.

The bouncer will fight alongside the Maelstrom gangers, who will be doing everything they can to focus fire their targets. They 'kinda' try to avoid collateral damage, but accidents do happen. If the fight starts seeming particularly dicey for the Maelstrom gangers, they will rush backstage to get the Maelstrom Psycho in initiative.

The bartender rushes back to the cooler area and barricades the door. Jerry will instantly flee like the rest of the civvies.

pages.name: So, uh... what DID happen to Clarisa?

text.content:
Though information about her sudden seizure is scattered around in the beats throughout this mission, here's the SparkNotes version:

  1. Clarisa got some bad Blue Glass.

    a. Her dance instructor, Aaron Kelly/Antonio, is planning a wild XBD (eXplicit/illegal Brain Dance) with women who come to his studio.

    b. To make them susceptible to the insanity he has in mind, he's hypnotizing/brainwashing them, KGB style

    c. To make them susceptible to activation, they need to be on his version of Blue Glass.

  2. The braindance getup helps make it happen, through poorly-understood means.

  3. The other part of the activation is PARTY ALL NIGHT, which was mistakenly released early/played in the Afterlife at the time of Clarisa's seizure.
